seo.title: Tool Loop Detection in AI Agents in LlamaIndex Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix tool loop detection in ai agents in LlamaIndex agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: Tool loops in LlamaIndex AI agents occur when the agent repeatedly invokes the same tools or retrievers without progressing toward query resolution, often due to ambiguous tool responses or irrelevant retrieved contexts. This manifests uniquely in LlamaIndex through infinite cycles between `query_engine.query()` calls and tool executions within `ReActAgent` or `OpenAIAgent` patterns. These loops consume excessive tokens and timeout, leading to degraded performance in production RAG pipelines.

howItManifests.scenario: ReAct agent cycling between query_engine and tool calls
howItManifests.symptom: Repeated calls to `self.query_engine.query()` with identical or similar inputs
howItManifests.impact: Token burn >10k per query, timeouts after 60s

howItManifests.scenario: Router agent stuck selecting same tool repeatedly
howItManifests.symptom: `agent.chat()` logs show identical tool selection pattern
howItManifests.impact: No final answer produced, infinite loop until max_iterations

howItManifests.scenario: Custom tool with non-terminating response format
howItManifests.symptom: Tool returns ambiguous JSON causing agent to re-query
howItManifests.impact: 100% failure rate on specific query patterns
Response quality drops to 20% accuracy, high latency

detection.name: Token Usage Pattern Analysis
detection.description: Detects loops by monitoring repeated tool calls exceeding token thresholds in LlamaIndex agent traces

detection.name: Query Engine Call Frequency Monitor
detection.description: Tracks `query_engine.query()` invocations with input similarity detection

detection.name: Agent State Cycle Detection
detection.description: Monitors ReAct agent state transitions for cyclic patterns

fixes.name: Max Iterations Circuit Breaker
fixes.description: Configure LlamaIndex agent with strict iteration limits and early termination

fixes.name: Tool Response Canonicalization
fixes.description: Standardize tool outputs to prevent ambiguous agent reasoning

fixes.name: Deduplicated Query Engine Wrapper
fixes.description: Cache and deduplicate query_engine calls within agent execution

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Tool Loop Detection in AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automatically instruments LlamaIndex agents to detect tool loops by analyzing execution traces, token consumption patterns, and state transitions in real-time. When loops are detected, Vex applies circuit breakers, reroutes to simplified query engines, or injects termination signals without code changes. The platform learns from loop patterns across deployments to preemptively harden agents against common failure modes like repeated `query_engine.query()` calls or tool selection cycles.

Integration requires wrapping the agent's `chat()` or `query()` method with `vex.verify()`, which captures full execution context including tool calls, retrieved nodes, and LLM reasoning traces. Vex's ML-based anomaly detection identifies loops within 2-3 iterations, typically preventing 95% of infinite loops before they consume excessive tokens.
vexAutomation.code:
import vex
from llama_index.core.agent import ReActAgent

# Initialize Vex
vex.init(project="llama-agent-prod")

# Your LlamaIndex agent
agent = ReActAgent.from_tools(tools, llm=llm)

@vex.verify(
    guards=[
        "tool-loop-max-iterations:5",
        "token-burn-high:10000",
        "query-repeat-similarity:0.8"
    ],
    remediation="terminate-early"
)
def safe_agent_chat(query: str):
    """Vex-protected agent execution"""
    return agent.chat(query)

# Production usage
response = safe_agent_chat("Analyze Q3 sales data")
# Vex auto-detects/prevents loops

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install with `pip install llama-index vex-sdk` and wrap your agents to eliminate tool loops in production.
